name: Alan Broadbent
description: New Zealand jazz pianist, arranger, and composer
abstract: Alan Leonard Broadbent  (born 23 April 1947) is a New Zealand jazz pianist, arranger, and composer known for his work with artists such as Sue Raney, Charlie Haden, Woody Herman, Chet Baker, Irene Kral, Sheila Jordan, Natalie Cole, Warne Marsh, Bud Shank, and many others.

interviews.title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The hard thing was that Alan Broadbent had written the arrangements and he knew what was going on and I was looking at them for the first time. But we had a rehearsal but only one. And then the next day we started recording
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: my friend Alan Broadbent was a winner that very same year and he came over on a boat from New Zealand to Berklee to come to school. So that’s when I met Alan Broadbent.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: So we recorded it with a big orchestra arranged and conducted by Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Woody always thought that too you know. They were all going for Bill Evans’ time, he says, “Well I had a guy that did that in 1944 you know.” Telling Alan Broadbent, he says, “Who’s that?” He says, “Jimmy Rowles .”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: Your friend and sometimes bandmate Alan Broadbent... especially if you’re working with a big band or orchestra like Alan does a lot and I do from time to time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: I'm speaking now about people like Alan Broadbent... And being able to take an old piece of material... to me, that was a personal triumph for him and for me.
